title: alsophila australis subsp. norfolkensis
intro: Alsophila australis subsp. norfolkensis, also known as the Norfolk tree fern, is a subspecies of tree fern native to the island of Norfolk Island in the Pacific Ocean. The Norfolk tree fern has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and support respiratory health. Herbal preparations of the Norfolk tree fern include teas, infusions, and salves, although modern research on its effectiveness is limited, and more studies are needed to confirm its medicinal properties.
